Tā shì wǒ de péng yǒu.

grammar_points.title: Using 是 (shì) - The verb 'to be'
grammar_points.explanation: 是(shì) is used to link the subject with a noun or pronoun. It works like 'am/is/are' in English. Formula: Subject + 是(shì) + Noun/Pronoun. For negative form , add 不(bù) before 是。 Formula: Subject + 不是(bú shì) + Noun/Pronoun

examples.pinyin: Wǒ bú shì zhōng guó rén

grammar_points.title: The possessive particle 的 (de)
grammar_points.explanation: 的(de) shows possession, like 's or 'of' in English. Formula: Possessor + 的(de) + Thing.

exercises.question: How do you say 'his book' in Chinese?

他 (tā) ___ 名字 (míngzi) 叫 (jiào) 鲁迅 (Lǔ Xùn) — His name is Lu Xun

grammar_points.title: Question particle 吗 (ma)
grammar_points.explanation: 吗(ma) is added at the end of a statement to turn it into a yes/no question. Formula: Statement + 吗(ma)？

exercises.question: Turn this into a question: 你是老师。

Tā shì ma nǐ de péng yǒu?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Tā shì nǐ de péng yǒu ma?
